name: Pact JS (Consumer-Driven Contract Testing)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Pact Foundation; PactFlow (hosted broker) is commercial

auth.notes: PactFlow hosted broker uses API key; self-hosted broker is free

performance.notes: Test execution; contract verification adds minimal overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Consumer-driven contract testing framework. Consumer defines interaction expectations (PactV4 DSL), generates pact JSON file. Provider verifies against pact. Pact Broker (or PactFlow) stores pacts and enables can-i-deploy gate. HTTP and message (Kafka, SNS) contracts. Rust core (pact_ffi) in v10+. Matchers: like(), eachLike(), term(), integer(), decimal(). WireMock/MSW alternative for mocking but Pact adds verification on provider side. Multi-language: JS, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, .NET.

gotchas: High setup cost — requires Pact Broker for full value
Provider-side tests require running the service
best_when: Microservices teams needing verified API contracts between services
Distributed teams where consumer-provider deploys are independent
can-i-deploy CI gates preventing breaking API changes
Organizations using PactFlow for contract management
avoid_when: Monorepo or tightly-coupled services (integration tests simpler)
Simple API mocking without provider verification
Small teams without dedicated contract testing workflow
summary: Pact JS implements consumer-driven contract testing — consumers define API expectations, providers verify them. Pact Broker stores contracts and enables can-i-deploy CI gates. HTTP and message (Kafka) contracts. Rust-core v10+. Multi-language support. Best for microservices teams needing verified, living API contracts across independent deployments. High setup cost — use WireMock/MSW for simpler mocking without provider verification.
